# Calorie Tracker - Project Context

## Overview
A vanilla JavaScript calorie tracking application with local storage persistence, chart visualization, and meal logging capabilities.

## Tech Stack
- **Core**: Vanilla JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5, CSS3
- **Storage**: localStorage API
- **Charts**: Chart.js (via CDN)
- **Icons**: Font Awesome (via CDN)
- **Build**: None (no bundler required)

## Architecture
- **Single Page Application (SPA)**: View-based navigation without page reloads
- **State Management**: In-memory state synced with localStorage
- **Performance Optimizations**:
  - DOM element caching
  - Date formatting memoization
  - Debounced storage writes (300ms)
  - Chart re-render prevention when data unchanged
  - O(n) favorite lookups using Sets
  - Batched DOM updates via DocumentFragment

## Key Files
- `index.html`: Main entry point and HTML structure
- `style.css`: Application styling
- `app.js`: Main application logic and state management
- `utils.js`: Utility functions (date formatting, calculations)
- `storage.js`: LocalStorage abstraction with debouncing
- `chart.js`: Chart.js configuration and rendering logic

## Core Features
1. **Meal Logging**: Add/edit/delete meals with calories, protein, carbs, fat
2. **Daily Tracking**: View daily totals and macro breakdowns
3. **Weekly Charts**: Visualize calorie trends over time
4. **Favorites**: Quick-add frequently logged meals
5. **Goals**: Set daily calorie and macro targets
6. **Persistence**: All data saved to localStorage

## Data Models
```javascript
// Meal object
{
  id: string,          // UUID
  name: string,        // Meal name
  calories: number,    // Total calories
  protein: number,     // grams
  carbs: number,       // grams
  fat: number,         // grams
  date: string,        // ISO date string
  isFavorite: boolean
}

// Daily summary
{
  date: string,        // YYYY-MM-DD
  totalCalories: number,
  totalProtein: number,
  totalCarbs: number,
  totalFat: number,
  meals: Meal[]
}

// User goals
{
  calories: number,
  protein: number,
  carbs: number,
  fat: number
}
```

## Performance Considerations
- Avoid repeated DOM queries by caching references
- Memoize expensive date formatting operations
- Debounce localStorage writes to reduce I/O
- Skip chart updates when underlying data hasn't changed
- Use Sets for O(1) favorite meal lookups
- Batch DOM insertions to minimize reflows

## Build Info
- Bundle size: ~43 KB (13.3 KB gzipped)
- No build step required - runs directly in browser
- Compatible with modern browsers (ES6+ support required)
